reasoning.typography: Generic sans-serif system with no character—could be any fintech. Headline weight insufficient for dominance, body copy unremarkable.
reasoning.hierarchy: Headline exists but doesn't dominate; weak contrast between h1/h2. Email input competes with headline. Navigation clutter dilutes focus.
reasoning.distinctiveness: Absolute stencil fintech—swap 'Mercury' for 'Brex/Ramp/Relay' and nothing changes. Hero image is AI-generated stock metaphor (desk-in-nature absurdism) with zero brand specificity.
reasoning.color: Restrained palette (gray, blue accent, muted hero tones) shows some discipline, but blue CTA is generic fintech palette. Accent usage appropriate at ~5%.
reasoning.density: Upper third is sparse in predictable fintech way—giant hero waste. Uniform padding, timid content density. Not confident, just following the template.
reasoning.voice: Pure stencil IA: claim ('Radically different') → vague promise → email capture. No actual story structure. 'Radically different' while looking identical to 47 competitors is almost parody.

winning_patterns: Clean contrast between text and hero image
anti_patterns: AI-generated hero image screams 'we hired a prompt engineer not a designer'
Claiming 'radically different' while executing generic fintech template
Headline typographic weight insufficient to dominate—CTA button competes visually
Stencil IA: Products/Solutions/Resources/About dropdowns could be any B2B SaaS
Zero brand personality—completely interchangeable with competitors
Wasteful density—massive hero real estate for minimal content payoff
Superscript ¹ on 'banking' is fussy legal-ese breaking typographic flow
